---
title: "Loops and Logics"
author: 
  - name: Lindsay Hayes
    url: https://lindsaynhayes.github.io
    affiliation: Department of Cell Biology, OUHC
date: 2026-07-17
draft: false
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# Pre-lecture activities

Before class, read the [pre-reading materials](/readings/index_R06.qmd):

# Lecture

## Learning objectives

::: callout-note
### Learning objectives

**At the end of class you will be able to:**

-   Identify when a loop can benefit your analysis pipeline. 
-   Distinguish between scalar and vectorized conditional logic in R and select the appropriate tool. 
-   Apply tools to recode column data based on single or multiple rules across the data.
-   Understand when to apply a column iteration or a row iteration. 

:::

## Slides

-   [Lecture 06: For Loops & Logic Statments](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1Cbpp-wkWYC38NzphxTLbpbmllb17gq1I3e5x9PxxvNQ/edit?usp=sharing)

-   [In-Class Activity](/activities/classwork06/classwork_L06.qmd)

# Post-lecture Practice

If you want to learn more read about iterations and functions in these books: 
[R for Data Science](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/iteration.html)
[DiSCDOWN](https://discdown.org/rprogramming/loops.html)
[An Introduction to R](https://intro2r.com/loops.html)
